title: cistus albidus subsp. varius
intro: Cistus albidus subsp. varius, also known as white rockrose, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Cistus albidus subsp. varius have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, respiratory problems, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, making it a versatile remedy for various health concerns.
